data.text: I’ll inspect the targeted e2e spec and the existing worker UI/data flow before editing, then wire the change to the current patterns and commit only the intentional files. I’m using the frontend-design skill because this bead changes an interactive UI surface.

I’m gathering the worker spec and app structure now, especially the existing GraphQL client and any current worker response component. That will tell me whether this is a targeted component patch or needs subscription plumbing.

The spec is under `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e`, and there are unrelated skill-directory changes already present in the worktree. I’ll leave those alone and only stage the worker UI files I touch.

The current detail panel has the right rough UI but it only renders initial `recentEvents`, separates all text from all tool cards, and subscribes only to log-line progress. I’m checking the generated schema/types and subscription helper next so the live stream frames can be represented without guessing field names.

I’m going to patch the detail page to keep its own live event stream, coalesce adjacent text frames, always expose the named live-response region, and bridge reconnects by refreshing `worker(id).recentEvents`. I’ll also make the log query tolerant so these worker-detail e2e mocks only need to mock the worker detail contract.

The live panel is now always present for the worker detail, with reconnect state and catch-up logic. I’m running the Svelte checker first because that will catch syntax, reactive-rune, and type issues before the browser tests.

`bun run check` can’t start yet because frontend dependencies are not installed in this worktree (`svelte-kit` is missing). I’m checking the package layout and lockfiles so I can use the repo’s expected install/test path without touching unrelated files.
The frontend expects `bun install` in `cli/internal/server/frontend`; I’ll install dependencies there, then rerun the checker and the targeted worker e2e spec.
The checker is clean for errors; it only reports existing warnings plus the explicit `role="region"` the bead requires. I’m running the workers e2e spec now to validate US-086a.a-c in the browser.

The first e2e run exposed two concrete issues: the backdrop’s accessible name collides with the close button, and the US-086a mocks are still letting one GraphQL request hit `/graphql`, which prevents the detail route from rendering. I’ll inspect the Playwright error context, then patch the route matching/loader behavior rather than broadening the tests.

I added static-preview fallbacks for the parent workers layout queries, which the US-086a tests intentionally don’t mock, and removed the close-button accessible-name collision. I’ll rerun the checker and then the worker spec.
The prior failed Playwright run left the preview server bound to port 4173. I’m going to identify and stop that leftover test server, then rerun the same spec.

The failing run was served by another concurrent preview on the shared default port; my built output has the patched `Dismiss panel` label and live region. The port is clear now, so I’m rerunning the worker spec once more against this worktree’s build.

The e2e runner is still producing snapshots from a stale worker chunk even though the current build contains the new live section. I’m going to start this worktree’s preview on an isolated port and inspect the served bundle directly, then use that to separate a code issue from the shared-port test harness issue.

The isolated preview confirms the feature code is loaded: 11 of 12 workers tests pass, and the remaining failure is just Playwright not exposing `<summary>` as `role=button`. I’m making the collapsible summary explicit for assistive tech and the test.

The summary label now fronts the path basename (`read prd.md …`), which matches the existing role query while still showing full inputs in the card body. I’ll rebuild the isolated preview once more and rerun the worker spec.

The isolated workers spec is green. I’ve stopped the temporary preview server and removed the temporary Playwright config; now I’m doing the final checker/status pass before staging only the intended files.

Formatting and the checker are clean for errors; the remaining warnings are the explicit ARIA roles required by the bead plus unrelated existing warnings. The shared default e2e port is clear now, so I’ll rerun the normal workers spec once before committing.

The normal Playwright run was affected by another short-lived process on the shared 4173 port: early tests passed, three mid-run navigations hit `ECONNREFUSED`, then the US-086a tests passed after this worktree’s preview came up. The port is clear again, so I’ll retry the normal command once.
The tracked generated `build/index.html` churn from local builds is back to clean; only the three intended source files remain. I’m staging those paths explicitly and committing the bead work.

Committed `0c9fc907 feat: stream worker live response cards [ddx-778fcfd1]`.

Verification:
- `bun run check` passed with 0 errors.
- `e2e/workers.spec.ts` passed 12/12 against an isolated preview port.
- The normal shared-port run hit a 4173 preview race, but US-086a.a-c passed once this worktree’s preview was serving.

Unrelated pre-existing skill-directory changes remain unstaged.
